summary: Reconcile code to the package/assembly model ÔÇö `template`Ôćĺ`package`, embed built-in packages from `.temper/packages/` (retire `contracts/`), custom kinds under `.temper/kinds/`

per.section: Decision: a package is project-authorable, not vendor-privileged ÔÇö and is itself a kind

gate.reason: the self-application `.temper/packages/` + `.temper/kinds/` surface was parked at cb52cc3 pending this reconciliation; a human must un-park it (author temper's built-in packages on temper's own surface) and settle the migration sequencing before the code can embed from `.temper/packages/` and retire `contracts/`

new.description: The authored built-in package sources (clauses + guidance), on temper's own composition surface ÔÇö the embed target that replaces `contracts/*.toml`. Human/dogfood territory (the parked surface, cb52cc3); the build embeds these into the binary as the shipped std-lib (`specs/10-contracts.md`, the `contracts/` retirement).

The `package` kind ÔÇö a package is itself an artifact of the `package` kind, checked by the definition (admissibility). A future plan tick decides whether this lands as its own module or folds into `contract.rs`; scoped here only to name the shape, not to fix it.

edit.description: Embed the built-in packages from `.temper/packages/<name>/` (a directory of authored sources) instead of `include_str!("../contracts/skill.anthropic.toml"|"../contracts/rule.toml")` at main.rs:59/65; wire the `package` kind into `gate()`'s admissibility. Embedding a directory needs a build approach (include_dir / build.rs codegen) ÔÇö a sanctioned-crate call folded into the fork.

Assembly binding by `package = "<name>"` per kind (replacing `adopt = "..."`); requirement typing facet `contract` Ôćĺ `package` (roster.rs / RequirementContract); rename the `template`/bundle vocabulary to `package`; custom kinds authored under `.temper/kinds/<name>/` and *registered* in the assembly rather than inlined under `[kind.<name>]` (`specs/40-composition.md` Decision rejects inlining the whole definition).
Requirement typing `contract` Ôćĺ `package` vocabulary and the contract-side conformance pass wording; the `RequirementContract::Template(path)` variant becomes a package binding by name.
Retire the last `template` reference; `contract` stays the require-side clause-set term (spec keeps `contract` distinct from `package`).
`template` Ôćĺ `package` vocabulary in the publishable-bundle path (`specs/50-distribution.md`, project-authored packages publish through `bundle`).

tests.asserts: assembly binding by `package`, requirement typing by `package`, and built-in packages resolving from the embedded `.temper/packages/` set; the `package` kind passes admissibility; snapshot churn across the renamed vocabulary is re-accepted.

acceptance: Code uses `package`/`assembly` per the spec's naming rule (`specs/90-spec-system.md`); built-in packages are authored under `.temper/packages/<name>/` and embedded (the `contracts/` dir retired); requirement typing uses `kind`/`package`; custom kinds are authored under `.temper/kinds/<name>/` and registered in the assembly; the parked self-application surface un-parks and `temper check` runs green on temper's own `.temper/`; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Large, entangled migration across compose/contract/roster/main/bundle ÔÇö NOT parallel-safe; a future plan tick decomposes it into a serialized (blockedBy) chain once `(package-surface-sequencing)` resolves. `lock.toml` rename already shipped. Blocked on the human un-parking `.temper/` (cb52cc3) ÔÇö the code must embed authored package sources absent from disk. `contract` (require-side clauses) stays distinct from `package` (the bundle); only the bundle term migrates.

summary: Custom-kind units join coverage ÔÇö read authored `satisfies` in `kind::Unit` instead of hardcoding it empty

per.section: Coverage ÔÇö the one referential check

gate.reason: follow-on; no story yet demands custom-kind coverage participation, and it is downstream of the `.temper/kinds/` custom-kind authoring in PACKAGE-MODEL-RECONCILE ÔÇö deferred on priority

edit.description: Populate `Features.satisfies` for a custom-kind `Unit` from its authored representation rather than the hardcoded `satisfies: Vec::new()` (the commented gap at kind.rs:451-454, inside `Kind::extract`), so a custom kind (temper's own `spec` corpus) can opt into requirements ÔÇö coverage is kind-blind by design. This likely needs a new `satisfies` extraction primitive (a deliberate algebra addition, `specs/15-kinds.md`).
A custom-kind unit declaring `satisfies` covers a requirement, exactly as a skill/rule does.

tests.asserts: a custom-kind unit's `satisfies` participates in coverage (kind-blind).

acceptance: A custom-kind unit's authored `satisfies` participates in coverage exactly as a skill/rule does; temper's own `spec` corpus can opt into requirements; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: kind.rs:451-454 hardcodes `satisfies=Vec::new()` in `Kind::extract` (confirmed on disk). Coverage is kind-blind (spec) so the seam is legitimate ÔÇö deferred on priority. Reading a custom unit's `satisfies` needs a projection + read (import_custom_unit at import.rs:409 writes a provenance-only meta.toml today) and likely a new extraction primitive (a deliberate algebra addition, `15-kinds.md`). Downstream of the `.temper/kinds/` custom-kind authoring in PACKAGE-MODEL-RECONCILE.

summary: Ship acquisition channels ÔÇö npm optionalDependencies binary wrapper, Homebrew, cargo install ÔÇö auto-detected, fail-loud on a missing platform binary

per.section: Decision: acquisition rides the ecosystem's package managers

gate.reason: needs human release setup (npm org, Homebrew tap, signing/publish creds) before the mechanical wrappers can be validated end-to-end

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages, GitHub release assets, and the Homebrew tap; channel auto-detected.

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`, confirmed on disk). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages (Biome's pattern) plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, `specs/50-distribution.md`). Reconcile with the flume manifest (a non-private publish entry, or a split package dir) rather than blindly clobbering the harness manifest.

acceptance: A stranger can acquire the prebuilt binary via npm (platform-specific optionalDependencies), Homebrew, standalone release assets, or `cargo install`; a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip.
notes: Release infra, mostly outside `src/`; the cargo gates can't validate npm/Homebrew publish, and channels need human creds (npm org, tap, signing). NB: root `package.json` already exists as the *private* flume manifest ÔÇö the npm-distribution config must reconcile with or split from it, never blindly create a second. Parked until a human sets creds up. `(binary-bootstrap)` RESOLVED ÔÇö execution, not a fork.

summary: Add the `agent` built-in harness kind ÔÇö `.claude/agents/*.md`, typed frontmatter + byte-faithful body, checked by an authored agent package

gate.reason: the reframe deprioritizes more built-in kinds; revive only if a story demands the agent kind ÔÇö and it lands only after PACKAGE-MODEL-RECONCILE reshapes how a kind binds its package

new.description: The Agent IR ÔÇö the typed model for a Claude Code subagent at `.claude/agents/<name>.md`, mirroring `src/rule.rs` end-to-end: typed YAML frontmatter (`name`, `description`, optional `tools`/`model`) with unknown keys preserved in `extra`, a byte-faithful body, and `provenance`. Identity is the path (file stem), not an internal field.

The agent kind's authored package (clauses + guidance) under temper's own surface, embedded ÔÇö NOT a `contracts/agent.toml` (the retired embedded-contracts model, PACKAGE-MODEL-RECONCILE). Clauses: `name` required + `allowed_chars` `[a-z0-9-]`; `description` required; `forbidden_keys` for the Cursor `.mdc` keys Claude Code ignores (`globs`/`alwaysApply`); an advisory `max_lines`.
Fixture-driven proof mirroring `tests/rule_contract.rs`: one broken agent per clause proving it fires, a clean agent that trips nothing, and an insta import round-trip proving idempotence.

edit.description: Add `pub mod agent;` beside the other artifact-kind modules (this entry owns the `pub mod` line).
Extend `Workspace` with `agents: Vec<Agent>` loaded under `<dir>/agents/*`, and add an `Agent(#[from] AgentError)` arm to `WorkspaceError`. Update the load tests.
Discover `.claude/agents/*.md`, project each to `<into>/agents/<name>/{meta.toml, AGENT.md}`, and add an `[[agent]]` lock row per agent ÔÇö parallel to the rule path.
Wire the `agent` kind into `gate()`: bind the embedded agent package, extract `agent_features` over `ws.agents`, extend the admissibility + conformance streams, add `"agent"` to `by_kind`.

tests.asserts: Each decidable clause of the agent package fires on its broken fixture and stays silent on a clean agent; import of an unchanged agent is idempotent (insta snapshot).

acceptance: `temper check` dispatches `.claude/agents/*.md` to the embedded agent package (conformance) and that package passes admissibility; `temper import` scans, projects, and round-trips agents idempotently with `[[agent]]` lock rows; cargo fmt/clippy -D warnings/test all green.
notes: Deferred by the requirements/satisfies reframe: more built-in kinds is the wrong direction. Now also downstream of PACKAGE-MODEL-RECONCILE ÔÇö the agent package is an authored `.temper/packages/agent/` artifact (embedded), not the retired `contracts/agent.toml`; binding follows the reconciled assembly model. Mirrors the rule kind end-to-end; on revival re-check its shared-surface blast radius (lib/check/extract/import/main) serializes against any then-open entry.
